steps.title: Treat the repeated group as the target
steps.content: The question asks for $2-4x$, so do not expand it. Let the repeated group stay together while you move terms.

steps.title: Isolate the group
steps.content: Start with $$5-7(2-4x)=16-8(2-4x).$$ Subtract $5$ from both sides: $$-7(2-4x)=11-8(2-4x).$$ Add $8(2-4x)$ to both sides to get $$2-4x=11.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The target expression has value 11. This is faster than solving for $x$ because the problem asks for the grouped expression itself.
